skip_reason: Inherits the same Triton-launch-overhead × 16-tiles × decoder-depth multiplier as --triton mode, which timed out at 3 h without producing a PNG. Op-by-op dispatch removes CompiledSequence's caller-side optimization, so wall-clock would be ≥ --triton (no fusion to amortize launches). Skipping this measurement saves another ~3 h of GPU time without changing the verdict — the bottleneck is structural Python launch overhead in the wrapper layer, not selection of compiled vs sequential.
scope_note: Same as triton above — gap closes under P-TRITON-FUSED-KERNELS (level 2 of R33).
